perturbation.original_text: The Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell the products of the Company, including the Snotarator™ Nasal Aspirator, ("Products") listed in the current "Price List" (Exhibit "A" attached hereto). The distribution right shall be limited to all countries and all of their territories and possessions within the continent known as South America ("Territory") (Exhibit "B" attached hereto).
perturbation.changed_text: The Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell the products of the Company, including the Snotarator™ Nasal Aspirator, ("Products") listed in the current "Price List" (Exhibit "A" attached hereto). The distribution right shall be limited to countries specifically authorized in writing by the Company, which may or may not include all or part of the continent known as South America ("Territory") (Exhibit "B" attached hereto).
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of "Territory" to allow the Company to selectively authorize countries within South America, we create an in-text contradiction. Originally, the Distributor had exclusive rights to *all* countries in South America, per Section 1.01. Now, that exclusivity is undermined, as the Company can limit the territory at its discretion. This contradicts the initially granted "exclusive right" and creates ambiguity about the Distributor's actual sales territory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distribution Right. The Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell the products of the Company, including the Snotarator™ Nasal Aspirator, ("Products") listed in the current "Price List" (Exhibit "A" attached hereto). The distribution right shall be limited to countries specifically authorized in writing by the Company, which may or may not include all or part of the continent known as South America ("Territory") (Exhibit "B" attached hereto). Distributor agrees that the Products, including the name Snotarator ™ Nasal Aspirator (the "Brand") are the sole property of the Company and Distributor has no interest whatsoever in such Brand and Products, and Distributor shall use the Brand and Products only for so long as the rights granted hereby remains in full force and effect. Distributor shall not take any actions, or aid or assist any other party to take any actions that would infringe upon, harm or contest the proprietary rights of Company in and to the Brand and Products. Distributor shall not assign the rights granted in this Section 1.01 without the prior written consent of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the Distributor exclusive rights to sell in all countries within South America. The changed text limits the territory to countries specifically authorized in writing by the Company, which may or may not include all or part of South America. The contradicted text section, which mirrors the changed text, still includes the term 'exclusive right'. However, the Company's ability to selectively authorize countries contradicts the very nature of an 'exclusive right' within the initially defined territory. The distributor no longer has exclusivity, since the company can authorize another distributor, which contradicts the word 'exclusive'.

perturbation.original_text: Terms are net cash upon delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: Terms are net cash upon delivery unless otherwise specified in a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction to the payment terms. Originally, Section 1.03 clearly states 'Terms are net cash upon delivery.' By adding 'unless otherwise specified in a separate agreement,' we now create uncertainty about the payment terms. It is unclear whether the 'net cash upon delivery' term is consistently applied, or whether the company can make a different payment agreement. This allows for selective enforcement or ambiguity, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Terms are net cash upon delivery unless otherwise specified in a separate agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contingency ('unless otherwise specified in a separate agreement') to the original payment terms ('Terms are net cash upon delivery'). This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, also located at 'Terms are net cash upon delivery unless otherwise specified in a separate agreement.', creating uncertainty about the payment terms and allowing for possible selective enforcement or ambiguity. This creates a structural flaw and introduces an in-text contradiction, as the payment terms are not clearly defined. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition where a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, seemingly one location but conflicting with itself) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Company warrants that Distributor shall acquire Products purchased hereunder free and clear of all liens and encumbrances except for Company's purchase money security interest defined in Section 1.03, above. Company further warrants all Products to be free from defects in material or workmanship under normal use and service for a period of ninety (90) days from the date of delivery. Any defects must be replaced by the Company within sixty (60) days within this scope of the warranty and all charges for labor and material, will be borne by Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Company warrants that Distributor shall acquire Products purchased hereunder free and clear of all liens and encumbrances except for Company's purchase money security interest defined in Section 1.03, above. Company's warranty coverage is subject to change without notice. Company further warrants all Products to be free from defects in material or workmanship under normal use and service for a period of ninety (90) days from the date of delivery. Any defects must be replaced by the Company within sixty (60) days within this scope of the warranty and all charges for labor and material, will be borne by Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'Company warrants all Products to be free from defects in material or workmanship under normal use and service for a period of ninety (90) days from the date of delivery.' By adding the clause 'Company's warranty coverage is subject to change without notice,' creates uncertainty about the warranty's validity. This undermines the clearly defined warranty period, as the company can unilaterally alter or revoke warranty coverage at any time, thus creating in-text contradiction. This could lead to disagreements regarding the scope and duration of the warranty coverage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Products Warranty. Company warrants that Distributor shall acquire Products purchased hereunder free and clear of all liens and encumbrances except for Company's purchase money security interest defined in Section 1.03, above. Company's warranty coverage is subject to change without notice. Company further warrants all Products to be free from defects in material or workmanship under normal use and service for a period of ninety (90) days from the date of delivery. Any defects must be replaced by the Company within sixty (60) days within this scope of the warranty and all charges for labor and material, will be borne by Company. If it is determined that either no fault exists in Company, or the damage was caused by negligence of Distributor, its agents, employees or customers, Distributor agrees to pay all charges associated with each such replacement. THIS CONSTITUTES THE SOLE WARRANTY MADE BY COMPANY EITHER EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED. THERE ARE NO OTHER WARRANTIES EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED WHICH EXTEND BEYOND THE FACE HEREOF, HEREIN, INCLUDING THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. IN NO EVENT SHALL COMPANY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES AND DISTRIBUTOR'S REMEDIES SHALL BE LIMITED TO REPAIR OR REPLACEMENT OF NONCONFORMING UNITS OR PARTS. 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the clause 'Company's warranty coverage is subject to change without notice.' This directly contradicts the original warranty provision that specifies a 'period of ninety (90) days from the date of delivery' as it allows the company to unilaterally alter or revoke the warranty at any time, thus creating uncertainty and conflicting implications for the warranty's scope and duration. The original clause gives a definite timeline of 90 days for the warranty, while the added clause removes the certainty by allowing for change without notice, leading to in-text contradiction as per the Structural Flaws definition.
